rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9252 and instead paid her own creditor claim without court authority. 2) Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed an allowance/rejection of the $113.61 creditor claim. Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed proof of satisfaction of the $113.61 creditor claim. 3) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9831 and did not receive court confirmation to settle alleged claims. Patricia Hand originally characterized these payments as preliminary distributions when questioned now alleges they are settlement payments made without required court approval. 4) Patricia Hand Amaro used estate funds to provide her current counsel with a retainer. Does not explain how much of a retainer was given to the attorney. 5) Court to determine how to proceed on the disclaimers.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2021 line 1. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: 1)Settling And Approving Receiver'S Final Accounting And Report; 2)Discharging Receiver; And 3) Exonerating Receiver'S Bond is continued to December 9, 2021. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THELMA M. THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Finding A Private Fiduciary
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The attorney for the petitioner as proposed successor personal representative of this estate concurrently represents the resigning executor/conservator of the decedent's spouse, as well as the personal representative of the probate estate of the decedent's spouse. The conservator of the decedent's spouse continued to report 100% ownership of the Naples property as an asset of the conservatorship estate, even after opening this probate to recognize the decedent's 50% ownership of the Naples property. The Court has already ordered distribution of 100% ownership of the Naples property from the conservatorship estate of the decedent's spouse to the personal representative of the probate estate of the decedent's spouse. The attorney has filed a "consent to multiple party representation" signed by the petitioner/proposed successor personal representative of this estate and resigning personal representative of this estate/conservator of the estate of the decedent's spouse. The personal representative of the probate estate of the decedent's spouse has not filed her consent to the attorney's concurrent representation of the petitioner as successor personal representative of this estate. Roosevelt Jr., who is not a beneficiary of this estate, but is a beneficiary of the estate of the decedent's spouse, has not filed his consent to the attorney's concurrent representation of the petitioner and personal representative of the estate of the decedent's spouse. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY MAZZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Due to the shortage of Court filing clerks, this third amended petition, while file stamped 11/10/21, was not actually E-filed until 11/16/21, when it would have been available for viewing. Notice mailed before the actual filing of a pleading is insufficient. (It is unclear who all of the people on the service list are, when the executor is reportedly now the sole beneficiary of this estate and only one Request for Special Notice has been filed.) If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] As noted previously, a petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each (here, the sole) beneficiary, not just "as set forth in the" petition. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] This must also be set forth in the order. Is an omnibus clause consistent with the terms of the settlement agreement? What if other property is discovered, title to which is not clear? The settlement agreement seems to address only the corpus of the [known] probate estate, since reference is made to "the entirety of the post-administration corpus" but not to any after discovered assets. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Rev. & Tax. Code Sect. 19513 was repealed. There is no CCP Sect. 366. If a person against whom an action may be brought dies before the expiration of the applicable limitations period, and the cause of action survives, an action may be commended within one year after the date of death, pursuant to CCP Sect. 366.2, but a creditor's claim against a decedent or her estate is subject to the claims filing period in Probate Code Sect. 9150.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM G. SIMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/28/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) Item 1 of the DE-120 form must specify the petitioner's representative capacity after his name. 4) Notice to Robert Sims and Keith Sims must be given to their place of business or residence. Probate Code section 1220(a)(3). Such addresses must also be confirmed by a verified statement. 5) A copy of the escrow closing statement for the sale of the estate real property must be filed. 6) A financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed with a declaration re retention of the original required by LRSF 14.35(D) & 14.60(A). 7) The petition alleges compliance with section 1064(a)(5), but Schedule C shows no interest payment after the real property was sold. Did the petitioner place the net proceeds from the sale in interest-bearing accounts? 8) The ending cash balance exceeds the FDIC limit. If the cash is held at a single institution, a proof of segregating and transferring the excess cash to insured accounts at additional institutions must be filed. 9) For each IAEA action taken by the petitioner for which notice of proposed action was required, all information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be provided. 10) Who was paying expenses to maintain the estate real property (property taxes, insurance, utilities, etc.) before the sale? Does the petitioner anticipate any reimbursement claim against the estate? 11) The proposed distribution schedule must provide a specific amount of net cash, as of a date certain, distributable to each beneficiary and assignee, after payment of the statutory fees and reimbursable costs. LRSF 14.36(I)(6). 12) An allegation as to whether there are outstanding bills due to a third party for preparing the account must be made. LRSF 14.36(I)(6)(a)(iii). 13) According to the petitioner's supplement filed on 11/04/2020, Linda Ryans is incapacitated. Please identify the conservator of her estate or other legal representative authorized to receive distribution of her share and provide a copy of documentation evidencing the authority. *An amended petition or an amendment to the petition will be required to address the issues, and new notice must be given. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELIZABETH F. COOPER TRUST F/B/O ANN R. MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #23, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Trustee is requesting fees of $45,750 reduced by investment advisory fees of $6,265.00. Examiner requested a legal basis/ legal authority for calculating the fees on an amount other than the 1% fair market value of assets at the end of the account period and how this calculation conforms to LRSF 14.53. No legal authority or legal basis has been provided. No explanation for how in conforms to LRSF 14.53 provided. The fair market value of assets at the end of the account period is $67,848.57. Fees based on 1% FMV are $678.48. Timesheets allege 50.5 hours were spent @ $600.00 per hour. Trustee is requesting $2,700.00 in fees for time from 01/06/2021 to 01/27/2021 that was already determined in the prior account. 2) Attorney requests fees of $7,765.50 which includes 8 hours/$3,720.00 in anticipated/unearned services. 11/09/2021 Supplement Exhibit A alleges $1,488.00 in fees earned from 9/13/2021 to 10/13/21.
